threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You insist on taking the bus over walking a few blocks, thinking it’s quicker. Locals know it’s just a few minutes of complaining about delays.
Caught the 53 last night, a guy was loudly arguing with his reflection in the bus window. Maybe he lost a bet or just needed a friend.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy casually repairing his bicycle while holding a coffee and chatting with the stray cats under the U-Bahn station. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab place that opens at 4 AM, just in time for the post-club crowd. Haven't seen that anywhere else!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they don’t bat an eye at a dog on the BART tracks but still complain about Muni delays like it’s a daily ritual.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a squirrel casually crossing the tracks at Civic Center today, while Muni was stalled for a dog. Only in SF do these delays come with a side of wildlife.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the bars close at midnight, but real locals know the best ones don’t even get lively until 1 AM.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the 5-minute wait for a train; locals have already set up camp with a coffee.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still root for the Overground over the Tube, but have no clue why everyone moans about the Dalston Junction steps.
Caught the N45 last night; a guy was singing opera at the back. I've never seen so many people just smiling, like they’d forgotten it was 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized it when the bakery on the corner started selling fresh bagels every Sunday, and I saw the same faces lined up each week.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar that serves cocktails mixed with homemade gummy bears—truly a taste of Berlin's quirky charm.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows to dodge the dog on the tracks at Civic Center—it's like a game of Frogger with a side of smoke and chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni at Civic Center today, missed my stop 'cause a guy was juggling on the aisle. Just another day in the TL, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think they can just pop into a bar for a 'quick drink'—meanwhile, we locals savor our vermut for hours, turning it into a mini fiesta.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell the newcomers by their vibrant, mismatched outfits—only locals know that black is the unofficial color here year-round.
